---
format: html
title: NAT and IPv6
description: Network Address Translation (NAT) and the transition to Internet Protocol version 6 (IPv6)
date: 2026-03-11
image: /images/15-ipv6.png
---

#### Preparation

- $4.3.3$ Network Address Translation (NAT)
- $4.3.4$ IPv6


#### Lesson Objectives

- Understand the concept of Network Address Translation (NAT) and its role in allowing multiple devices to share a single public IP address.
- Learn about the structure and features of IPv6, including its larger address space and improved header format.
- Understand the different types of IPv6 addresses (unicast, multicast, anycast) and how they are used.

Bonus Objectives:

- Learn about the transition mechanisms from IPv4 to IPv6, such as tunneling and dual-stack deployment.
- Understand the role of middleboxes (e.g., firewalls, NAT devices) in the context of IPv6 and how they affect end-to-end connectivity.
